claim: Current AI infrastructure buildout is 100x larger than the internet expansion of the late 1990s/early 2000s
actionable: Plan infrastructure investments with the understanding that AI requires unprecedented scale; expect persistent scarcity of compute, power, and network for 3-5 years.
source_title: Building the Real-World Infrastructure for AI, with Google, Cisco & a16z

claim: Power is the limiting constraint in AI infrastructure expansion - data centers are being built where power is available, not where ideal locations are
actionable: Consider power availability as the primary factor in deployment location decisions; geographic diversity of compute is driven by power availability.
source_title: Building the Real-World Infrastructure for AI, with Google, Cisco & a16z

claim: GPU/TPU demand exceeds supply dramatically - 7 and 8-year-old hardware runs at 100% utilization, and companies are turning away high-value use cases due to compute scarcity
actionable: Accept that compute scarcity will persist; optimize for efficiency and multi-tier compute strategies using diverse hardware generations.
source_title: Building the Real-World Infrastructure for AI, with Google, Cisco & a16z

claim: Specialized architectures (TPUs for ML, custom silicon for serving/agentic workloads) are 10-100x more power-efficient than general-purpose processors
actionable: Invest in or adopt specialized hardware for specific workloads; the power savings justify custom silicon development timelines.
source_title: Building the Real-World Infrastructure for AI, with Google, Cisco & a16z

claim: Specialized chip development cycles are 2.5 years minimum; geopolitical factors (China vs. US chip access) will drive regional architectural divergence
actionable: Plan long-term chip strategies accounting for geopolitical constraints; expect different optimal architectures by region.
source_title: Building the Real-World Infrastructure for AI, with Google, Cisco & a16z

claim: Networking will become a primary bottleneck; workloads have burstiness causing 100MW+ power demand swings that utilities notice
actionable: Design systems that optimize network efficiency; even small network improvements multiply computing power availability.
source_title: Building the Real-World Infrastructure for AI, with Google, Cisco & a16z

claim: Scale-out commodity computing (rather than mainframe consolidation) remains the architecture despite large unified systems because jobs have varying sizes
actionable: Maintain flexibility in cluster allocation; don't consolidate all workloads on single supercomputers since different jobs need different scales.
source_title: Building the Real-World Infrastructure for AI, with Google, Cisco & a16z

claim: Hardware and software must be co-designed (like BigTable/Spanner with cluster architecture); the computing stack will be unrecognizable in 5 years
actionable: Plan for deep collaboration between hardware and software teams; don't assume hardware or software choices made today will remain optimal.
source_title: Building the Real-World Infrastructure for AI, with Google, Cisco & a16z

claim: Inference cost reduction of 10-100x is already happening; however, users keep demanding higher quality models which negate efficiency gains
actionable: Accept that inference cost per token continues to drop but absolute costs may not - measure value by capability improvements, not just unit economics.
source_title: Building the Real-World Infrastructure for AI, with Google, Cisco & a16z

claim: Prefill and decode phases of inference require different hardware optimization; ideally you would use different hardware for each phase
actionable: When building inference infrastructure, consider optimizing separately for prefill (throughput) and decode (latency) phases.
source_title: Building the Real-World Infrastructure for AI, with Google, Cisco & a16z
